programme.desc: Phobia expert Dr Robin Zasio comes to the aid of Katie, who is afraid of spiders and will go out of her way to secure every nook and cranny against them. The programme also features Andrews fear of cockroaches and Jackies fear of butterflies

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson installs a stainless steel kitchen in the trees for a family of sweet-makers. The treehouse also boasts barrel ceilings and a loft bedroom

programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy offers his guidance on a cat struggling to control its bladder in a multimillion-dollar dream home. Plus, one aggressive feline bullies another

programme.title: Dogs: The Untold Story
programme.desc: The canine documentary explores the training of dogs to serve in challenging roles, including their use on the front-lines of war and their skill in medical detection

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Brett Raymer and Wayde King try to turn a pinball machine into a state-of-the-art aquarium, and discover if a high-tech robot can function inside a series of tanks

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Charles Jantzen seizes some emaciated horses that were forced to eat dirt to survive. Two of the mares have foals and are struggling to produce milk to feed them

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: An emaciated horse gives birth prematurely, and the foal is so weak Dr Thurston takes it home to bottle feed

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys come to the aid of a North Carolina couple whose cabin is so ramshackle they have exposed wires in the shower and their livestock has been half-eaten by predators

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Two puppies diagnosed with swimmers syndrome take their first steps, with help from Tania and Mariah. Marcel rescues a stray dog trapped beneath a house

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.desc: The staff develop an innovative course of treatment when a cheetah has an injury. Plus, the zoo hosts the only pair of platypuses outside Australia

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.desc: The Rhino Rescue Center welcomes Arthur, the southern white rhino calf. Meanwhile, a young nyala must be hand-raised before returning to its herd

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: The wardens are extremely busy during prime deer season. Bad weather conditions lead to a serious car crash and illegal deer disposals are investigated

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff performs surgery on a dog that cannot keep its food down and weighs 20 pounds less than it should. A cat is brought in with a bloody wound on its side

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson travels to Japan with treehouse builder Takashi Kobayashi, who takes the presenter on a tour of his greatest creations, including a bar in Tokyo

programme.title: The Pool Master
programme.desc: Anthony Archer-Wills presents the best moments from the series, as well as never-before-seen footage and behind-the-scenes antics from the first three episodes

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: The Kilchers are just recovering from a brutal winter and a massive earthquake, but now severe weather puts them and their livestock at risk

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Jeremy and his sons search for a lost bulldozer in the frozen wilderness, and Mark plans to show his new fiancee the beauty of McCarthy, but finds danger

programme.title: Alaska Monsters
programme.desc: A team of outdoorsmen takes on the challenge of exploring the Alaska Triangle to uncover proof of the cryptic creatures thought to be living there

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: Arctic temperatures and a hungry grizzly wreak havoc on a struggling homestead. Marty and Misty take action with big builds including a blacksmith shop- but then tragedy strikes

programme.title: Great Hammerhead Invasion
programme.desc: Leading shark scientist Tristan Guttridge tries to find out why, every November, the usually solitary giant hammerhead sharks congregate off the coast of Florida

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Two tastemakers hire Pete Nelson to create a cool treetop structure with a wall of windows and a crows nest

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: A massive dog fighting operation leads the Pennsylvania SPCAs Humane Law Enforcement team to exhume the front yard of a property

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: The team closes down a cat shelter to save the lives of the surviving occupants after finding some already dead

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: The splintering that occurred under the Stuart kings, as political, religious and cultural divisions fuelled war across three kingdoms. Architect Amanda Levete climbs the Tulip Stairs in the Queens House, Greenwich, and reassesses Inigo Jones elegant and innovative design, while portrait artist Tai Shan Schierenberg encounters Van Dycks monumental portrait of the Earl of Pembrokes family, and finds signs of the dysfunction and tensions which point to the civil war to come

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes turns her attention to Socrates, detailing how the Athenian philosopher secured a reputation as an influential maverick. She also highlights how his contributions as one of the founders of Western philosophy did not please his detractors, as his outspoken defence of his beliefs ultimately led to his execution

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant concludes his exploration of the French Rivieras artistic legacy by focusing on how art and celebrity became intertwined in the early part of the 20th century. Resident artists such as Picasso and Matisse found the sun-drenched landscape ideal for experimenting with their work, and as the region became a creative hub, the likes of novelist Jean Cocteau and ballet impresario Sergei Diaghilev were drawn to the area. This artistic community also liked to party on the Cote dAzurs beaches - a pastime that proved equally irresistible to the American multimillionaires arriving in the area on board transatlantic liners

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Adventurer and explorer Paul Rose heads for Wensleydale as he sets out to explore the region, starting in the small town of Hawes, where locals now run many of their own services, including the buses. Paul checks out why cycling is so popular in the Dales and tries to get to the top of the Buttertubs Pass. Along the way he meets a family of dormice and spends time with a man who looks after one of the areas most imposing castles

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming battles his way through blanket weed as he travels along the Shropshire Union Canal and discovers industrial secrets in Audlem, Cheshire

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Ancients
programme.sub-title: The Vanished Capital of the Pharaoh
programme.desc: The vanished ancient Egyptian capital Piramesse, constructed 3,000 years ago at the command of pharaoh Rameses the Great. The city sparked confusion when it was found in a location where it could not possibly have been built. Archaeologists Manfred Bietak and Edgar Pusch try to solve the mystery. Narrated by Mark Halliley

programme.title: The 1951 Festival of Britain: Brave New World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how the 1951 Festival of Britain helped the nation to look forward to a brighter future during a period of post-war austerity. Archive footage and personal testimonies reveal how, despite widespread debt and rationing, the event encouraged people to have fun through design and ingenuity

programme.title: British Art at War: Bomberg, Sickert and Nash
programme.sub-title: Paul Nash - The Ghosts of War
programme.desc: Presenter Andrew Graham-Dixon explores how three British artists - David Bomberg, Walter Sickert and Paul Nash - responded to the cataclysm of the First World War. He begins by focusing on the work of Paul Nash, who sketched the battlefields of Flanders near Ypres on 25th May 1917. He was so fixed on his work that he tripped and fell into a trench, breaking his ribs - an accident that went on to save his life and influence his later work

programme.title: An Art Lovers Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke discover the art and architecture of Barcelona, seeking out the Romanesque frescoes that inspired a young Miro and the designs of an engineer who arguably put more of a stamp on the city than its star architect, Antoni Gaudí

programme.title: The Jet Stream and Us
programme.desc: Documentary examining the influence that the jet stream - a ribbon of fast-moving air high up in the atmosphere - has on everyday weather. Here, scientists explain the effects the phenomenon has had on climate and discuss what could happen should it change its pattern of behaviour

programme.title: The 1951 Festival of Britain: Brave New World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how the 1951 Festival of Britain helped the nation to look forward to a brighter future during a period of post-war austerity. Archive footage and personal testimonies reveal how, despite widespread debt and rationing, the event encouraged people to have fun through design and ingenuity

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: The splintering that occurred under the Stuart kings, as political, religious and cultural divisions fuelled war across three kingdoms. Architect Amanda Levete climbs the Tulip Stairs in the Queens House, Greenwich, and reassesses Inigo Jones elegant and innovative design, while portrait artist Tai Shan Schierenberg encounters Van Dycks monumental portrait of the Earl of Pembrokes family, and finds signs of the dysfunction and tensions which point to the civil war to come

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Adventurer Paul Rose heads for Swaledale, joining farmers who look after rare upland hay meadows and enters the cake-making contest at Muker Show using a pressure cooker he has taken on a climb of Mount Everest. Paul also goes underground to try and find rare industrial artefacts left behind by lead miners in the 19th century and meets All Creatures Great and Small star Peter Davison

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming gets stuck in the mud in Woodseaves Cutting, and explores the charming canal-side village of Kinver in Staffordshire. First broadcast 9 December 2020

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth decides that she and husband Richard should have a weekend at a golfing hotel so that she can keep up with the Major and his wife. Roy Clarkes suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy attempts to put his dreams into practice as the annual promotion interview looms - by taking up sleepwalking. Ronnie Corbett stars

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: A chronicle of the English Civil War, revealing that behind the romantic stories of Cavaliers and Roundheads lies a terrible tale of tragedy and the death of innocents. The conflict saw hundreds of thousands of men, women and children killed, families torn apart and the nation divided, culminating in the public execution of King Charles I and the rise of Cromwell as Lord Protector

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: The story of how Dave Tomkin, originally a safe-cracker from London, rose from small-time criminal to international mercenary and arms dealer enabling death and destruction around the world. Now, at the end of his notorious career and life, he reflects on his past, the people he worked with and the lives lost along the way

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: The history of the Empire State Building, commissioned by General Motors as a rival to the Chrysler Building, with personal accounts from a steelworker involved in its construction and, in a story which remarkably mirrors the tragic events of September 11, a widow whose husband died in 1945 when his plane crashed into the 79th floor. The programme also explores the life of public relations king Al Smith, the former governor of New York who once represented the public face of the impressive building, the worlds tallest for 40 years

programme.title: The Lost Neanderthals
programme.desc: Following an international team of scientists as they discover and investigate precious relics of the Palaeolithic period, resulting in groundbreaking revelations about the human populations of around 50,000 years ago. The researchers uncover some of the secrets of the Mandrin Cave in Southern France, where the remains of a Neanderthal was uncovered in 2015

programme.title: An Art Lovers Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke discover the art and architecture of Barcelona, seeking out the Romanesque frescoes that inspired a young Miro and the designs of an engineer who arguably put more of a stamp on the city than its star architect, Antoni Gaudí

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Ancients
programme.sub-title: The Vanished Capital of the Pharaoh
programme.desc: The vanished ancient Egyptian capital Piramesse, constructed 3,000 years ago at the command of pharaoh Rameses the Great. The city sparked confusion when it was found in a location where it could not possibly have been built. Archaeologists Manfred Bietak and Edgar Pusch try to solve the mystery. Narrated by Mark Halliley

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Home Improvement Company Lies Cost Me Thousands
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate the case of the homeowner who paid a company nearly £2,000 up-front for garden maintenance, but has nothing to show for it. In the first of a new series of How-To Guides, consumer expert Harry Kind explains how to find a tradesperson you can trust

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin looks at a down-at-heel 1930s Nottingham bungalow thats been transformed into a modern, stylish house. Martel Maxwell and Jacqui Joseph report on developers in Workington, in Cumbria, and Gillingham, in Kent, as they work wonders on their auction buys

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Gary Pe assist the teams at an antiques centre near Exeter, and Christina Trevanion meets a museum curator with a collection of shipwrecked Lego pieces

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Chepstow, friends Ella and Issy race to turn charity shop finds into profit, taking bold risks on furniture and fashion before a tense live-streamed auction

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Interior designer Banjo Beale and his team are brought in to help two small businesses who survive by selling their produce via honesty boxes. Mary runs a small sandwich shop on Mull and is too busy with family matters to open the shop all day and wants to make her honesty box stand out and attract visitors. In South Uist, in the Outer Hebrides, Donna currently runs the honesty box for her ice-cream from an old livestock trailer and wants Banjo to build something more imaginative and fun

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a family from the coastal city of Southend-on-Sea find a Suffolk home with a holiday let, then stops by a boatyard which restores historic boats

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Firefighter Lisa wants to become a signwriter in the Scottish Highlands, and blacksmith Menelik is looking for financial security before proposing to his girlfriend

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: An intrepid team of four competes for cash by crossing a bridge of stepping stones across the studio floor. Quiz show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Antisemitism: Why British Jews Are Afraid - Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: The search for Northern Irelands Greatest Garden is almost at an end - five passionate gardeners have reached the final. After an early autumn road trip spent revisiting the five incredible gardens, Carol and Diarmuid join the finalists, their families and friends at Greenmounts walled garden in County Antrim. The pair reveal which one of the five will be named the winner

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Cat has to make a speech during the racecourses Ladies Day event, and it forces her to confront what shes been denying about her relationship with Dylan. With alcohol and adrenaline in the air, its not long before rivalries are stirred up and best behaviour gives way to chaos. Shannon and Arran are caught in the crossfire as their families violently collide

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Dylan rages at Shannon in the wake of Ladies Day, but she escapes and spends a life-changing day with Arran. Meanwhile, tensions erupt between Cat and Dylan, making them question the foundations of their life together. Can they find a way to salvage what theyve built, and will the family survive what comes next?

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan joins officers from the Police Service of Northern Ireland as they patrol the streets of Belfast. Response officers Anna and Perri race to the aid of a man who has had bleach squirted directly on his face and into his eyes. Across town, Tom and Nigel race to the scene of a serious crash that has a left a pedestrian badly injured

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Sue Perkins hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedian Finlay Christie and journalist Camilla Long joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Brothers Iain and Kirk Russell from Glasgow present Suzie Fletcher with a leather football made famous by their grandfather, Rangers player Ian McMillan. It was used in the Scottish Cup final replay between Rangers and fierce local rivals Celtic back in 1963, a game that Rangers won 3-0. Bookbinder Chris Shaw is asked to restore a magazine written by the owners father when he was being held in a Japanese PoW camp, Geoffrey Harvey restores a pachinko machine - a pinball-style device - for a pair of sisters, and Rebecca Houghton works on a lyra - a bowled folk instrument - from Crete

programme.title: The Woman in Black (2012)
programme.desc: A lawyer tormented by the death of his wife is sent to a remote village to put deceased client Alice Drablows affairs in order. The locals are suspicious of outsiders and determined to keep the areas tragic history a secret, and the late Mrs Drablows isolated house appears to be haunted by a vengeful spirit that seems hell-bent on redressing a decades-past grievance. Horror based on Susan Hills novel, with Daniel Radcliffe and Ciaran Hinds

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Fake Loan Repayments Coming Off My Wages
programme.desc: Investigative journalist Shari Vahl takes on the case of the lorry driver fighting to clear his name after fake loan payments taken from his wages. Theres also a look at the eBay scam that ensnared two drummers at opposite ends of the country - leaving one of them with no drum kit and out of pocket

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A young developer in Bushey Heath, Hertfordshire follows his mothers advice while working on a ground floor flat and an estate agent in blown away by a house in Pontypool, Monmouthshire. Two friends turn a healthy profit from transforming a terraced house in Croydon, south London

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: At the Detling Showground in Kent, Stephanie Connell helps the reds tackle a military challenge, while John Cameron and the blues hunt for a music or drama-related item. The rivalry leads to plenty of friendly competition around the fair and dishes up some drama at the auction. Charlie Ross monitors progress and also visits nearby Lullingstone Castle, former site of the UKs first silk farm, to learn about some unusual former residents

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: Best friends and former Traitors stars Sonja and Aubrey travel to Harrogate, where they scour the the North Yorkshire towns 17 charity shops to raise money for a local cat sanctuary. Sonia adopts a disciplined approach, focusing on timeless, high-quality fabrics and classic tailoring, while Aubrey leans into bold style and personality - although presenter Joanna Page worries he may be falling into a common thrifting trap - choosing pieces he loves personally rather than thinking purely about profit

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo Beale and his team head to Iona to give a makeover to a wool and craft shop, with owners Mike and Kate hoping for a minimalist, Scandinavian look. Banjo comes up with a Hebridean modern design idea he hopes will warm up the shop, with a team of local tradespeople to help him realise his vision

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: After viewing more than 200 houses, a couple from the city of Sheffield are hopeful that Cornwall will finally deliver their dream countryside home, with enough space for their extensive collection of books and film memorabilia. . Briony May Williams takes on the challenge

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Textile waste upcycler Rukhsana wants to turn her hobby into a business but lacks the confidence to promote herself. Her mentor Zoe and Dom Chinea set her the biggest challenge theyve ever given any maker. Electrician Jan wants to swap his day job for a stone-carving career but needs help building the business that will allow him to switch careers

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Colleagues Steve, Sophie, Dean and Ady aim to win big by stepping in the right direction, avoiding the wrong answers and making it across the bridge to the other side. Game show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for a cash prize

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The Mitchells try to get through to Phil, Cindy and Max have a close call, and the Slaters surprise Big Mo

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The designers take over a medieval-style castle hotel where they are tasked with turning bedrooms into perfect love nests, drawing on their own ideas of what makes a romantic retreat for inspiration. They are able to reuse and repurpose the hotels existing furniture to show off their ability to upcycle and demonstrate their styling skills, before they are judged by Michelle Ogundehin and hotel design expert Linda Boronkay

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Amateur cooks including a dairy manufacturer, a medical writer and a maths student enter the kitchen to battle for one of four MasterChef aprons. They begin by producing their signature dish, before following a recipe for a fish finger open sandwich, served with tartare sauce and pickle slaw - the catch being parts of the recipe are missing. Finally, the pick of the crop cook for three diners who know a thing or two about MasterChef - previous winners Chariya Khattiyot and Steven Wallis, and former finalist Omar Foster. Presented by Grace Dent and Anna Haugh

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan joins officers from the Police Service of Northern Ireland as they patrol the streets of Belfast. Dave stumbles upon a drug den. Jade and Adam open up about the mental health scars of being a Peeler and Perri uses pepper spray to bring a violent suspect down

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: As Niall celebrates his wedding, he follows his estranged friend Ruben into a barn, away from all the guests - and in an explosion of violence, he brutally and shockingly attacks him. The story then goes back in time to reveal how two men, who formed such a close bond years before, came to such a point. Drama, written by and starring Richard Gadd, with Jamie Bell

programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Altered States
programme.desc: The documentary-maker explores the changing face of US culture, beginning with a look at a movement in Portland, Oregon, that seeks to broaden how the world views marriage in a time of rising divorce rates, suggesting that monogamy is not essential and that polyamorous or open relationships can be equally fulfilling. However, while Louis meets many who have found happiness with multiple partners, he also discovers tales of heartbreak and jealousy

programme.title: My Mates a Bad Date
programme.desc: Oloni has her work cut out with Vicky as she helps her find her perfect match, hoping to change her behaviour and her unrealistic criteria for her suitors

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: The former footballer heads deep into the Colombian jungle to find out why gold is now twice as valuable than cocaine to the countrys organised crime groups, meeting the young men drawn into this illegal venture, discovering the shocking health impact mining is having on them, as well as meeting the cartel gangsters who control almost every element of life in these remote regions

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Ashley heads to Sweden where he joins police at the front line of the ongoing gang war and meets with gang bosses in underground car parks. As trouble expands throughout the country, children as young as 13 are taking weapons and justice into their own hands, a photojournalist shows him their documentation of the murder scenes before he meets with a top gang leader for a training session involving pistol target practice in the dead of night

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey encourages members of the Patel family to tidy their immediate environment, as mum Bijal, dad Darshan and their two children move all their possessions into a warehouse, where they are encouraged to recycle, donate and sell items. There are emotional moments as the quartet go through their most treasured items. Bijal and Darshan struggle to part with their possessions, but really want to create an environment free of the chaos for their son, who finds it hard to concentrate in the clutter. As usual, home improvement gurus Dilly Carter, Iwan Carrington and Robert Bent offer tidying and upcycling tips

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Altered States

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Dark States
programme.sub-title: Louis Theroux: Dark States - Murder in Milwaukee
programme.desc: The reporter examines gun crime and racial tensions in the US city, where the homicide rate is 12 times the national average and the police are frequently regarded with distrust. He follows the police departments homicide officers as they investigate a shooting, spends time with a family that has lost a loved one to gun crime, and meets a former gang leader who has become a social activist dedicated to finding solutions to her citys problems. He also examines the impact of a recent police shooting

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Stacey continues to follow the emotional journey of the young women who have reported rape and now trying to navigate the justice system, she learns to appreciate that the statistics are stark. Fewer than two per cent of reported sexual assaults result in conviction, though this increases to over 50 per cent for cases that proceed to trial. As their trials near, the women learn that credibility also plays a pivotal role

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Ashley heads to the Philippines to find out if joining a gang is the only way to survive in the slum districts of the capital Manila. Determined not to fall short of their peers, the girls try to cram four years of fun into one night. Comedy directed by Olivia Wilde and starring Kaitlyn Dever, Beanie Feldstein and Jessica Williams

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Part one of two. The journalist and media personality meets four young women who have reported being raped by someone they know. With unprecedented access to women who have waived their right to anonymity after reporting being raped, Stacey spends three years following the complainants in the lead-up to trial. She explores the challenges of reporting rape when the alleged perpetrator is someone familiar, and asks whether the justice system delivers what those desperate to be believed hope for

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: The story of Kilmarnock nurse Adele Rennie, who pretended to be handsome, professional men to target, harass, stalk and exploit hundreds of women. When Dr David Graham starts flirting with former beauty queen Abbie Draper online, she turns sleuth and uncovers a trail of deception, crazy coincidences and stories that just dont add up

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth II: Her Story, Our Century
programme.desc: Documentary exploring a century of change in Britain through the prism of Elizabeth IIs life. Through archive and eyewitness reflections, the film explores how she led the monarchy through profound social, political and cultural change. With contributions from Helen Mirren, David Attenborough, Barack Obama, Tony Blair, and HM Queen Camilla

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: George Anderson plants a bed of new raspberry varieties in his fruit cage and propagates new growth from Beechgroves existing fig tree, while Ruth Vichos returns to the vertical garden that she started last year. Theres a back to basics guide from Colin Crosbies plot just outside Dumfries, and a review of the gravel and grass garden, which will be thinned and replanted over the course of this season

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: Joanna Page is in the West Yorkshire town of Hebden Bridge, where friends Emma and Sophie compete to raise money for Dove House Hospice, picking up items including a vintage St Michael jacket, a rare red rotary dial telephone, a 1970s Holly Hobbie doll and a colourful 1960s picnic set

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the hidden wild world of a country garden in Cumbria. A barn swallow returns to its birthplace to prepare a new nest, field mice sneak under the noses of predators, a newt lays eggs in the pond, and a mole barrels through his tunnel network in search of a female. But Cumbria is the wettest county in England, and when the weather breaks, things take a turn for the worse for the animals

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leeds United. Action from the second semi-final at Wembley Stadium . The Blues thrashed Port Vale 7-0 in the quarter-finals and are looking to lift the trophy for the first time since 2018. Leeds only triumph in this competition was back in 1972 and they defeated West Ham United on penalties in the previous round

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Tokyo on a sleek Shinkansen bullet train and explores the ornate Akasaka Palace, built in 1909 by Emperor Meiji for his son, the crown prince Yoshihito. On board a sleek Shinkansen bullet train, Michael arrives in Tokyo, one of the greatest cities on earth, with a population of 37 million in the metropolitan area. He begins by exploring the ornate Akasaka Palace, built in 1909 by Emperor Meiji for his son, the crown prince Yoshihito. Hes surprised to find that, with its neo-baroque architecture and impressive fountains, it looks more than a little British. In the fashionable district of Ginza, Michael journeys underground to ride the metro, where he is intrigued by the catchy jingles played at each station and meets one of the composers behind 300 of the tunes

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents live coverage of the evening session on day 10 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the second round concludes with the final frames of two matches. For the fifth time, all three members of the Class of 92 - Ronnie OSullivan, John Higgins, and Mark Williams - progressed to the quarter-finals in 2025, overcoming Pang Junxu, Xiao Guodong and Hossein Vafaei respectively. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Éadaí SOS
programme.desc: Caoimhe Ní Chathail and Proinsias Ó Coinn head to Swords in Co Dublin to offer fashion advice to Dáithí, who is always buying clothes but finds it difficult to achieve the look he wants. Proinsias and the team confiscate his clothes for further analysis, leaving Dáithí with just 15 items of clothing for a fortnight. And to make things more challenging, hes also on a shopping ban. In Irish

programme.title: Charmain and the Prophet
programme.desc: More than a decade on from Charmains death, reporter Myles Bonnar asks a veteran detective to review the case, and investigates Eric Adusahs past, uncovering hidden truths. With so many questions about what happened to Charmain, he tracks the so-called Prophet down in search of answers

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.sub-title: Pregnant and Jailed in Post Office Scandal
programme.desc: On her first day running her local Post Office, Seema discovered a cash shortfall at the till. She had to put in her own money to make the books add up. Every day, more and more money appeared to be missing. She didnt know it then, but she was the latest victim of the infamous Post Office Horizon accounting scandal - and eventually she was convicted and sent to prison, while pregnant. Here, Bill pairs her with artist Jack, whose detailed paintings explore resilience, as she tells her remarkable story

programme.title: Countryfile

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: At 1am in Cumbria, a 999 call reports a gang breaking into a supermarket cash machine using high-powered cutting tools. They work quickly, escaping with thousands before police arrive. Its the second ATM targeted in the region within 24 hours. Seventy miles away, news of the raids is shared with West Midlands Police, who are already pursuing a prolific gang linked to similar attacks nationwide. As the task force builds intelligence, Cumbria Police lead the forensic investigation, until a third raid heightens the urgency of the case

Endaf starts strongly, snapping up a quality blazer for under a tenner, while Margaret picks up Dutch wooden clogs and a vintage leather bag

programme.sub-title: Illegal Blood Sports and Tractor Sabotage
programme.desc: In South Yorkshire, farmer Helen Smith has endured years of intimidation from illegal off-road bikers tearing across her land. Now, with her elderly mother living in fear after shots were fired at their home, police are determined to track down the gang behind the violence. In Hampshire, officers pursue men suspected of terrorising wildlife and wrecking farmland by taking part in the illegal blood sport of hare coursing. Plus, reporter Paul Colgan discovers how farmers in the Scottish Highlands doubling as on-call firefighters are using cutting-edge technology to protect their rural communities

programme.title: Inside Britains National Parks
programme.desc: The Northumberland National Park is characterised by a unique landscape of wild moors, gentle hills and deep valleys criss-crossed by clear rivers, with its western border adjoining Kielder, one of the largest man-made forests in Europe. This vast natural expanse is a haven for numerous bird species, including the osprey, once extinct in the UK, but now returning annually from Africa to breed, while the park is also rich in history, including extensive stretches of Hadrians Wall

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts tells the story of Viking England by studying the history of York. She takes part in a Viking battle, meets the metal detectorists behind one of the UKs greatest archaeological finds and gets her hands on an almost 1,000-year-old poo. Aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson takes to the skies to see how the current layout of York reflects its history

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the Norman period of British history by examining events that occurred in Winchester, a historic town that still retains many hallmarks of the 11th and 12th centuries. To get a taste of this era, she attempts to storm a castle wearing chain mail, investigates the origins of modern surgery, and tries some eel pie. At the conclusion of the episode, cutting edge CGI is used to take the town back in time, and recreate how it would have looked during this period of British history

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada

programme.title: Dickinsons Real Deal

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: A stunning UAP - Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena - appears above Skinwalker Walker Ranch during an experiment involving hundreds of illuminated drones. The timing of what happens next leaves the team members in disbelief

programme.title: Beyond Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: In an effort to gain a broader perspective and build larger data sets, the Skinwalker team adds two team members and takes the offensive by pursuing evidence of similar phenomena found at analogous sites around the country

programme.title: Legends of the Pharaohs
programme.desc: Amenhotep III was a king who inherited the prosperous kingdom of Ancient Egypt in the 18th Dynasty. In a time of comparative peace, he dedicated his time to diplomacy and extensive building in Egypt and Nubia

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There: Military Mysteries
programme.sub-title: Ghost Armies, Missing Pilot, Lost Bomber, Inside the AC-130
programme.desc: Combat veterans analyse controversial wartime images, documents and recordings spanning global wars and modern conflicts, aided by experts to uncover mysteries and alleged cover-ups. This programme investigates the fate of a Desert Storm fighter pilot who was being shot down in Iraq and the survival of six Second World War pilots after their B-24 bomber crashed in the Libyan Desert

programme.title: UFO POV

programme.title: Strange Evidence

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service
programme.desc: Gordons late-night investigation of Mrs Whites Golden Rule Cafe turns up shocking results, and a bombshell reveals a secret financial crisis. Will the 101-year-old proprietor and her 75-year-old son Larry ever hand over the reins and retire?

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service
programme.desc: The chefs thorough late-night investigation of MacGregors unearths far scarier secrets than the ghosts that are rumoured to haunt the restaurants dining room - mouldy leftovers, frozen produce and the previous owners discarded belongings. Burdened by the past, and up to her neck in debt, new owner Stephanie allows head chef Mike, whos also her boyfriend, to cut corners in the kitchen. Can Gordon orchestrate a fresh start?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The family sees a different side to Grampa when he revisits his days as Gorgeous Godfrey - the most feared and villainous TV wrestler of all time. Mr Burns reveals he was a huge fan and persuades the old man to put on his spandex suit and return to the ring for one last fight

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A routine check reveals Homer is not qualified to work as a safety inspector at the power plant, so he goes back to college to study for a degree - but spends all his time playing pranks and ends up failing his exams

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Moe has a chance encounter with a couple of rich venture capitalists who invest in the barmans home-made whiskey. Grampa injures himself while babysitting Bart, but quite enjoys being looked after by his grandson, and Lisa attends a jam session at the Jazzhole - only to find none other than Bleeding Gums Murphy on the bill

programme.title: Outrageous Homes
programme.desc: Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen meets Trevor, whos 86 and has dedicated decades to creating his very own Venetian palazzo in Staines, from plunging light wells to vaulted ceilings and lavish waterways. On the Isle of Wight, Laurence takes a trip to Poppys kawaii home of ice-cream lights, doughnut sprinkles and cotton candy walls. The interior design expert also meets Sonia, whos been both thrifty and nifty in her maximalist mini mansion

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three pairs of partners from Edinburgh compete to win the £1,000 prize, including competitive couple Kenny and Lisa, who believe their dishes are good enough to be served in a restaurant. Later, duo Julie and Keith hope to get away with preparing two very similar courses, before final hosts Kate and Mike hope to impress their rivals with a Tex-Mex menu

programme.desc: Carla calls together her ex-classmates from St Cletes School for Wayward Girls to plot revenge on her sadistic former principal when she spots her drinking at Cheers. American bar-room comedy, starring Rhea Perlman

programme.desc: Diane rings Sam, hoping for a better offer after Frasier pops the question while they are in Italy. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, Shelley Long and Ted Danson

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: A desperate Ray does everything he can to get to the front of the queue to meet the New York Mets 1969 team at the National Baseball Hall of Fame. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tells Debra she takes up too much room in bed - but he comes to regret complaining when she banishes him to the couch. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.sub-title: The Ann Who Came to Dinner

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Cranes take a trip to the mountains for a holiday away from the city, but Frasier, Niles and Daphne are plagued by nightmares during the overnight journey. Martin also has a strange dream, but unlike the rest of the family, continues to sleep soundly

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles buys an expensive sweater, despite promising Daphne he would start saving for their childs college fund, and Frasier agrees to get Roz and Alice tickets to see a show by his ex-wife, which only serves to fan the embers of lust. Laurie Metcalf guest stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin suffers a mild heart attack, but tries to keep it from Ronee - to the dismay of Frasier and Niles, who feel it would be unfair of him to hide such serious news. Eventually worn down by their persistence, he decides to confide in her - leading to a conversation that ends in an unexpected way

programme.desc: Midday news summary

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Henry Cole meet Gaby, whose garden is strewn with interesting items. From a mass of tangled weeds, Henry pulls out a vintage sack barrow, while Simon finds a shepherds stove hidden in the undergrowth

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Matt, Janice and Mike head to historic Cragside house and gardens in Northumberland, the original smart home powered by ingenious 19th-century hydro-electric inventions. Janice and Matt get to grips with a Victorian dishwasher, while at Cragend Farm, the Bakers discover an agricultural machine which inspired the design of Londons Tower Bridge

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with the Rev Kate Bottley in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: BandB owners Andy and Julian enlist the help of Laura Hamilton in finding their dream home away from home in Gran Canaria on a budget of £125,000

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: The owners of a rural retreat in Andalucia prepare to host their first wedding, while in France, expansion is on the cards for Colin and Christina who left behind office jobs to set up a gîte and glamping business. In Italy, Jane and Walter face a difficult decision about their future at their unique BandB

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: In Brittany, Donna and Paul race to finish their utility room, while in Normandy at Château de Lalacelle, Terry and Ashley turn their attention to the front gardens, with ambitious water features planned and kilometres of pipework to lay. Meanwhile in the Dordogne, moving day brings a startling discovery for Helen and Malcolm: a cellar packed with 20,000 bottles

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: The Garners help Trevor and Asia find a place big enough for all their travel treasures, and the Hamiltons find Gene and Theresa a place with a bedroom that passes the shimmy test

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Dog behaviour specialist Sue pulls out all the stops for Woodgreens most rejected dogs. Barley the labradoodle hopes his latest liaison might prove to be third time lucky. Elsewhere, its a rollercoaster of emotions as beloved long-term resident Lady, a Staffie cross, finally has a chance of finding a home after a year of searching

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: Documentary following 12 adult virgins as they embark on a unique course in intimacy at a luxury retreat, to see if it can help them overcome the fears. The group meet for the first time and begin to unpack their deepest fears and anxieties. But with shame running deep and barriers high, can any of them open up and take the first step toward intimacy?

programme.title: The Worlds Tallest Man: The Next Chapter
programme.desc: Documentary about a man who was declared the tallest in the world in 2010, but continues to grow due to a rare condition that could kill him. Fifteen years later, this film explores his circumstances after life-saving surgery. Doctors examine the toll that his towering body has had on his health, while Guinness World Records measures him again to see if he still holds the title that made him famous

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Following three patients who have adapted to the challenges thrown their way, including 27-year-old Josh, who was rushed to AandE after being thrown from his motorbike at speed. Georgie has brought in her 10-month-old son Phoenix after she caught him sucking on a bleach tablet, while 21-year-old Ross is in AandE after dislocating his shoulder at work

programme.title: Rescue: Extreme Medics
programme.desc: The air ambulance is dispatched to two serious road traffic collisions, while on the west bank of Loch Lomond, a minibus carrying 15 tourists has collided with a car. In South Ayrshire, an 86-year-old grandmother is struggling to breathe after a high-speed crash, and 72-year-old William has a severe cut to his arm after an attempt to chop wood with a circular saw went horrifically wrong

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to change the fortunes of the Olde Stone Mill restaurant in Tuckahoe, New York, which is struggling to attract customers, leading to debts of nearly $1million. Gordon feels the owner is more interested in his own appearance than how his food tastes and a screaming match ensues

programme.title: Grayson Perry Has Seen the Future
programme.desc: Grayson concludes his tour of Silicon Valley by considering the motivations of the men behind the technology, such as Elon Musk, Mark Zuckerberg and Jeff Bezos - and how their values might be challenged. He meets a scientist working on empathetic robots which offer a more human-centric alternative to the priorities of the industry and encounters one of the key players in AI, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark, grappling with the possibility that AI could be shaped in such a way to offer a more optimistic future

programme.title: Your Song
programme.desc: In London, a 90-year-old sings the Frank Sinatra classic that gave him back his voice after he had a stroke. A 15-year-old performs an original tune about growing up Black in Britain. An 83-year-old from Derry sings a song that she gave up 30 years ago because it made her daughter cry, and a father and son perform the lullaby thats always been their song

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three sets of dinner-party hosts from Maidstone, Kent, compete, beginning with drama school owners Lisa and Iain, who cook an entirely Scottish menu. On the second night, Hannah and Paul fail to wow their guests with a Middle Eastern and Moroccan menu, while the final soiree sees Ling and Mark prepare food they like to call Chinglish, which combines Lings Chinese heritage and Marks English roots

programme.title: Jamies One-Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie cooks one-pan recipes that offer the perfect solutions for busy lives. Here he makes a dukkah roast lamb feast with a tip to speed up cooking

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: A shocking discovery for Ste forces a confrontation, Jack finds himself in the middle of his feuding relatives, and a teenager refuses to take orders from their mum. Meanwhile, at The Lofts club night, Mercedes is loved-up and Cleos ready to let off steam, and Rex goes public wearing womens clothes. Mercedes advises Theresa on how to handle Kathleen-Angel, Ro tries to comfort Rex, and tension sizzles between two former flames

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with the Rev Kate Bottley in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: The dearly departed Coachs old friend Woody comes to work at Cheers, while Sam is staggered to learn Diane has joined a convent and immediately sets off to convince her she has made a dreadful mistake. Woody Harrelson joins the cast

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: New barman Woody misses his girlfriend, so Sam and Diane arrange a surprise visit for him. Comedy, starring Woody Harrelson and Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: A coat left behind at the bar arouses Dianes curiosity and she dreams the owner could be her Prince Charming, so Sam insists she must go out with the man if he returns to claim it

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra spend a romantic weekend away, but try too hard to show each other a good time. Meanwhile, Marie spies on Robert, who has been left to babysit the children

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra gets a job, filling Ray with dread at the thought of having to take responsibility for all the household chores while she is at work

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra and Ray are taken aback to realise they are much nicer to strangers than to each other, and decide to show a little more mutual consideration in future - but their resolve soon crumbles. American comedy, guest starring Kevin James as Doug Heffernan, his character in The King of Queens

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Infuriated at the dullness of his life, Frasier steals a small box from a neighbour for the thrill, but guilt prompts him to look for a way to return it without being caught - a task that proves easier said than done. With the guest voice of Helen Mirren

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Daphne plan a natural birth, causing the father-to-be to develop sympathy pains. Meanwhile, Frasier hires a professional matchmaker - but ends up going on a string of bad dates. Guest starring Laura Linney

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles takes a sudden liking to fast food, Ronee alarms Martin by revealing her mother is coming to town, while Frasier pines for attractive matchmaker Charlotte, and heads for a singles bar to drown his sorrows. Comedy, guest starring Laura Linney and Jennifer Tilly, with Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frank almost gets in the way of Frasiers latest meeting with Charlotte, but the pair finally admit their feelings. Roz goes on a date with a jailbird and Martin plans to present Ronee with an old engagement ring - until Daphne points out how insensitive it would be

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay strives to turn around the fortunes of Puerto Rican restaurant El Cantito. The establishment, located in Yonkers, New York, has struggled to stay afloat post-Covid amidst the breakage of key appliances, staff problems and such unfortunate instances as uncooked chicken being served. Can Gordon turn things around?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In north Wales, Simon OBrien and Henry Cole meet Anne and her grandson Tom. Simon unearths an art deco fire guard

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers visit Englands original seaside resort, Scarborough - a favourite of Mikes from his youth and a place filled with fun, sun and history. The family pitch up at Scarborough Spa and are wowed by its continental-style Sun Court, the home of Britains last professional seaside orchestra. Down on the promenade, they head to the Harbour Bar, Yorkshires oldest ice cream shop

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by the Rev Kate Bottley in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lisa and Wayne got married on Corfu, in the town of Kassiopi. And now that theyre about to buy a holiday home, it was an obvious choice to head back there. Laura Hamilton shows them five properties for their £130,000 budget; will any of them impress these newly-weds enough for them to put in an offer?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: In rural France, theres a big win for foodies Scott and Elena and their catered breaks, after having their cycling tour company punctured by Covid-19, avid bikers Emmeline and David take to the Spanish roads with new ideas. In Italy, Carli and James attempt to overcome financial pressures at their holiday rental property

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: At Château de Lalacelle in Normandy, Terry and Ashley are expecting more than 1,000 visitors, but their front garden still looks like a building site. At Château de la Boutinière, Alison and Zion attempt to create a top-floor apartment, but a floating ceiling and a dismantled fireplace threaten to derail their plans, while Kathryn and Steve race to finish a vast kitchen makeover in Château dAzerat home before their son arrives

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: The Garners deliver an impressive villa for Mark and Julie, who are looking for their forever home in Almería. Brothers-in-law Graham and Norman are given food for thought in Alicante thanks to sisters Sophie and Charlotte Rodriguez. Away from the house hunts, Sharon Garners growing animal rescue - including mischievous new arrival Coco the macaw - keeps the family on their toes

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Before the Sunday school becomes a pottery studio, Keith Brymer Jones and partner Marj Hogarth have a significant landmark to celebrate first - Keiths 60th birthday. They turn the hall into a cabaret-style venue and invite friends, family and new neighbours to party with them, tasting home-made gin and bringing music back to the chapel with the help of a 50-strong male voice choir

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: Things are set to escalate as the group explore their core desires and lean into animal instincts. Berties readiness to fully embrace island life is called into question, Marianne works through deep-rooted issues of trust and some of the group take a significant step, beginning the most advanced form of therapy at the retreat - surrogate partner therapy

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service
programme.desc: Gordon arrives at Marvel Ranch in Pennsylvania, a family owner diner that has operated for more than four decades but is close to shutting its doors for ever. The owner Cheryl practically grew up in the restaurant but is on the verge of bankruptcy after buying the business from her father. Despite this, she continues to care to care for her dysfunctional staff, who are too busy messing around to give her anything in return. Can Gordon tame the brawling staff and transform the sloppy kitchen crew into proper chefs?

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of Britains favourite personalities hit the sofa to share their opinions of First Dates, Squid Game, Olivia Attwood: The Price of Perfection, Match Me Abroad, Building the Band and BBC News reports on the game of bingo and the return of Oasis

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The Essex crew and Herts Air Ambulance race to help a teenager, who has a major brain injury after colliding with a car in Hemel Hempstead. In Bicester, Dimis heart is racing dangerously fast and needs to be shocked back into a normal rhythm and in Clacton, some DIY goes wrong for Martin, who has a suspected broken collarbone

programme.title: Wonders of the World I Cant See
programme.desc: Blind comedian Chris McCausland explores some of the worlds most iconic landmarks to discover things that the ordinary traveller might miss. Chris is joined by Harry Hill for a trip to the Acropolis in the Greek capital Athens. They soak up some local culture, including sea fishing, pottery making, olive oil tasting and Greek drama lessons, and even try their hand at a few Olympic sports in the iconic Panathenaic Stadium

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Joe Lycett explores how to get the most out of a weekend away with a fun-packed, 48-hour itinerary of trademark humour and facts. This time, fellow comedian Sarah Millican joins him for an exploration of the Lithuanian capital Vilnius. The duo take to the skies to see the city from above - an experience they find by turns terrifying, thrilling and beautiful. Keen to explore Lithuanian cuisine, they sample cold beetroot soup and cepelini dumplings washed down with gira, a drink made from fermented beer. Later, the pair explore Vilnius medieval centre on foot, taking in Gediminass tower and Lukiskes Prison

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: Sandi and comedian, actor and writer Roisin Conaty explore Wales from the Pembrokeshire Coast to the English border staying in the best retreats the country has to offer. Starting with Seren Mor near the Nevern Estuary, Roisin is blown away by the contemporary architecture and dogs on the beach. Electric bikes take the pair over to Powys where they stay at the Hergest Lee Cabin and Lean-To, styled with quirky design and mid-century furnishings. Finally, the pair top off their escape in Moor Tower, a refurbished folly by Hay on Wye just a mile from the Welsh boarder boasting views of the Black Mountains, The Brecon Beacons and the Wye Valley

programme.title: Big Mood
programme.desc: In her excitement to grab an olive branch from Eddie, Maggie takes her drowsy night medication instead of her daytime tablets, making the task of being there for her friend infinitely more difficult. Comedy, starring Nicola Coughlan and Lydia West

programme.title: Big Mood
programme.desc: When Maggie reunites with her estranged famous dad, Eddie pressures her friend to let Whitney use him in a rainstick healing workshop for Instagram clout. Its all downhill from there. Robert Lindsay guest stars

programme.title: Rescue: Extreme Medics
programme.desc: The air ambulance is dispatched to two serious road traffic collisions, while on the west bank of Loch Lomond, a minibus carrying 15 tourists has collided with a car. In South Ayrshire, an 86-year-old grandmother is struggling to breathe after a high-speed crash, and 72-year-old William has a severe cut to his arm after an attempt to chop wood with a circular saw went horrifically wrong

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The family sees a different side to Grampa when he revisits his days as Gorgeous Godfrey - the most feared and villainous TV wrestler of all time. Mr Burns reveals he was a huge fan and persuades the old man to put on his spandex suit and return to the ring for one last fight

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: A routine check reveals Homer is not qualified to work as a safety inspector at the power plant, so he goes back to college to study for a degree - but spends all his time playing pranks and ends up failing his exams

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Moe has a chance encounter with a couple of rich venture capitalists who invest in the barmans home-made whiskey. Grampa injures himself while babysitting Bart, but quite enjoys being looked after by his grandson, and Lisa attends a jam session at the Jazzhole - only to find none other than Bleeding Gums Murphy on the bill

programme.title: Outrageous Homes
programme.desc: Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen meets Trevor, whos 86 and has dedicated decades to creating his very own Venetian palazzo in Staines, from plunging light wells to vaulted ceilings and lavish waterways. On the Isle of Wight, Laurence takes a trip to Poppys kawaii home of ice-cream lights, doughnut sprinkles and cotton candy walls. The interior design expert also meets Sonia, whos been both thrifty and nifty in her maximalist mini mansion

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three pairs of partners from Edinburgh compete to win the £1,000 prize, including competitive couple Kenny and Lisa, who believe their dishes are good enough to be served in a restaurant. Later, duo Julie and Keith hope to get away with preparing two very similar courses, before final hosts Kate and Mike hope to impress their rivals with a Tex-Mex menu

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George visits a property in Yorkshire which is part Victorian stone farmhouse and part brick extension, with interiors that are outdated and in need of renovating. Meanwhile in Buckinghamshire, George meets the owners of an 18th century gatehouse who need help changing the layout of their living room

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Highlights of the food and chat show, with Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by Paralympian Richard Whitehead in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Carla persuades Sam to hire an older woman to replace Diane, but it is not long before he turns his amorous attention to the new barmaids daughter. Comedy, starring Ted Danson

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Carla calls together her ex-classmates from St Cletes School for Wayward Girls to plot revenge on her sadistic former principal when she spots her drinking at Cheers. American bar-room comedy, starring Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Diane rings Sam, hoping for a better offer after Frasier pops the question while they are in Italy. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, Shelley Long and Ted Danson

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra takes Ally and the twins to her parents house, leaving Ray at home by himself - and he finds it impossible to get to sleep. American comedy, starring Ray Romano, Patricia Heaton and Peter Boyle

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: A desperate Ray does everything he can to get to the front of the queue to meet the New York Mets 1969 team at the National Baseball Hall of Fame. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray tells Debra she takes up too much room in bed - but he comes to regret complaining when she banishes him to the couch. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: The Ann Who Came to Dinner
programme.desc: Maris is released on bail and placed under house arrest, only to embroil an unwitting Niles in a madcap scheme to flee the country. Meanwhile, Frasier is forced to take care of Rozs insurance agent Ann. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer, David Hyde Pierce and Julia Sweeney

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Cranes take a trip to the mountains for a holiday away from the city, but Frasier, Niles and Daphne are plagued by nightmares during the overnight journey. Martin also has a strange dream, but unlike the rest of the family, continues to sleep soundly

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles buys an expensive sweater, despite promising Daphne he would start saving for their childs college fund, and Frasier agrees to get Roz and Alice tickets to see a show by his ex-wife, which only serves to fan the embers of lust. Laurie Metcalf guest stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin suffers a mild heart attack, but tries to keep it from Ronee - to the dismay of Frasier and Niles, who feel it would be unfair of him to hide such serious news. Eventually worn down by their persistence, he decides to confide in her - leading to a conversation that ends in an unexpected way

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Acerbic chef Gordon Ramsay steps in to save a small cafe and a couples engagement. Christopher Keff and his fiancee Tess Robinson inherited New York diner Love Bites Cafe, but working together has created a toxic work environment and has soured their relationship to the point that Gordon recommends therapy

programme.desc: Frank almost gets in the way of Frasiers latest meeting with Charlotte, but the pair finally admit their feelings. Roz goes on a date with a jailbird and Martin plans to present Ronee with an old engagement ring - until Daphne points out how insensitive it would be

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay strives to turn around the fortunes of Puerto Rican restaurant El Cantito. The establishment, located in Yonkers, New York, has struggled to stay afloat post-Covid amidst the breakage of key appliances, staff problems and such unfortunate instances as uncooked chicken being served. Can Gordon turn things around?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In north Wales, Simon OBrien and Henry Cole meet Anne and her grandson Tom. Simon unearths an art deco fire guard

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: The Bakers visit Englands original seaside resort, Scarborough - a favourite of Mikes from his youth and a place filled with fun, sun and history. The family pitch up at Scarborough Spa and are wowed by its continental-style Sun Court, the home of Britains last professional seaside orchestra. Down on the promenade, they head to the Harbour Bar, Yorkshires oldest ice cream shop

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray hosts the words-and-numbers quiz, with Rachel Riley taking care of the board and Susie Dent joined by the Rev Kate Bottley in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lisa and Wayne got married on Corfu, in the town of Kassiopi. And now that theyre about to buy a holiday home, it was an obvious choice to head back there. Laura Hamilton shows them five properties for their £130,000 budget; will any of them impress these newly-weds enough for them to put in an offer?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: In rural France, theres a big win for foodies Scott and Elena and their catered breaks, after having their cycling tour company punctured by Covid-19, avid bikers Emmeline and David take to the Spanish roads with new ideas. In Italy, Carli and James attempt to overcome financial pressures at their holiday rental property

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: At Château de Lalacelle in Normandy, Terry and Ashley are expecting more than 1,000 visitors, but their front garden still looks like a building site. At Château de la Boutinière, Alison and Zion attempt to create a top-floor apartment, but a floating ceiling and a dismantled fireplace threaten to derail their plans, while Kathryn and Steve race to finish a vast kitchen makeover in Château dAzerat home before their son arrives

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: The Garners deliver an impressive villa for Mark and Julie, who are looking for their forever home in Almería. Brothers-in-law Graham and Norman are given food for thought in Alicante thanks to sisters Sophie and Charlotte Rodriguez. Away from the house hunts, Sharon Garners growing animal rescue - including mischievous new arrival Coco the macaw - keeps the family on their toes

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Our Welsh Chapel Dream
programme.desc: Before the Sunday school becomes a pottery studio, Keith Brymer Jones and partner Marj Hogarth have a significant landmark to celebrate first - Keiths 60th birthday. They turn the hall into a cabaret-style venue and invite friends, family and new neighbours to party with them, tasting home-made gin and bringing music back to the chapel with the help of a 50-strong male voice choir

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: Things are set to escalate as the group explore their core desires and lean into animal instincts. Berties readiness to fully embrace island life is called into question, Marianne works through deep-rooted issues of trust and some of the group take a significant step, beginning the most advanced form of therapy at the retreat - surrogate partner therapy

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service
programme.desc: Gordon arrives at Marvel Ranch in Pennsylvania, a family owner diner that has operated for more than four decades but is close to shutting its doors for ever. The owner Cheryl practically grew up in the restaurant but is on the verge of bankruptcy after buying the business from her father. Despite this, she continues to care to care for her dysfunctional staff, who are too busy messing around to give her anything in return. Can Gordon tame the brawling staff and transform the sloppy kitchen crew into proper chefs?

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Following three patients who have adapted to the challenges thrown their way, including 27-year-old Josh, who was rushed to AandE after being thrown from his motorbike at speed. Georgie has brought in her 10-month-old son Phoenix after she caught him sucking on a bleach tablet, while 21-year-old Ross is in AandE after dislocating his shoulder at work

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Forty-two-year-old logistics manager Luke from Chichester is back in the UK after seven years working in the Middle East and is looking for a woman to reinvigorate his love life. Thirty-five-year-old amateur pole dancer Abi from Colchester is looking for fun times with someone silly who can send her heart into a spin

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: Nicolle and Bens next leap into non-monogamy is to try a couple swap. However, as the residents warm up for a sexually charged rendezvous in the retreats cloisters, social anxiety kicks in for a teary Nicolle. As she works through her nerves, a flirty evening with resident married couple Gage and Olivia leads to the bedroom. As the lights are dimmed, it remains to be seen whether Nicolles insecurities will re-emerge. Meanwhile, engaged couple Katey and Dan plan a night of sexual fun with residents Josh and Holly

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Joe Lycett explores how to get the most out of a weekend away with a fun-packed, 48-hour itinerary of trademark humour and facts. This time, fellow comedian Sarah Millican joins him for an exploration of the Lithuanian capital Vilnius. The duo take to the skies to see the city from above - an experience they find by turns terrifying, thrilling and beautiful. Keen to explore Lithuanian cuisine, they sample cold beetroot soup and cepelini dumplings washed down with gira, a drink made from fermented beer. Later, the pair explore Vilnius medieval centre on foot, taking in Gediminass tower and Lukiskes Prison

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: Sandi and comedian, actor and writer Roisin Conaty explore Wales from the Pembrokeshire Coast to the English border staying in the best retreats the country has to offer. Starting with Seren Mor near the Nevern Estuary, Roisin is blown away by the contemporary architecture and dogs on the beach. Electric bikes take the pair over to Powys where they stay at the Hergest Lee Cabin and Lean-To, styled with quirky design and mid-century furnishings. Finally, the pair top off their escape in Moor Tower, a refurbished folly by Hay on Wye just a mile from the Welsh boarder boasting views of the Black Mountains, The Brecon Beacons and the Wye Valley

programme.title: Big Mood
programme.desc: In her excitement to grab an olive branch from Eddie, Maggie takes her drowsy night medication instead of her daytime tablets, making the task of being there for her friend infinitely more difficult. Comedy, starring Nicola Coughlan and Lydia West

programme.title: Big Mood
programme.desc: When Maggie reunites with her estranged famous dad, Eddie pressures her friend to let Whitney use him in a rainstick healing workshop for Instagram clout. Its all downhill from there. Robert Lindsay guest stars

programme.desc: Three couples from Kent compete to win the £1,000 prize, including married West End performers Millie and Andy, who channel family roots to prepare a Greek Cypriot-inspired menu. programme.desc: The actor and his 20-year-old son Barney hit the road together, travelling more than 2,000 miles from Los Angeles to New Orleans. programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Actor Bradley Walsh and his 20-year-old son Barney hit the road together, travelling more than 2,000 miles from Los Angeles to New Orleans. In the second leg of their journey, the pair leave the city of Los Angeles behind and make their way down the famous Route 66 to the southwestern state of Arizona. Here, they ride through a deserted ghost town, before the father and son reach Lake Havasu City, where Barney has arranged for them to try jet-skiing. Finally, they attend a Cowboy College, where they learn to become real-life horsemen

programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Got High

programme.sub-title: The One with the Apothecary Table

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebes frank attitude causes Monica and Rachel to lose their tempers. Joey reluctantly accepts a job as a waiter at Central Perk, while Ross and Chandler argue over which of them made up a raunchy joke

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel gives advice on being more independent to her pampered younger sister (Reese Witherspoon), who promptly makes her regret it by flirting with Ross. Meanwhile, Joey gets in trouble for giving free food to female customers at Central Perk

programme.desc: Chandlers home-made gift for Monica lands him in trouble. Joey hires a lookalike to help him participate in a medical study, and Ross demonstrates martial arts to Rachel and Phoebe. Comedy, starring Matthew Perry and Courteney Cox

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain
programme.desc: Dermot Murnaghan examines the tragic case of five-year-old April Jones, who went missing on October 1 while playing with friends, and was last seen getting into a vehicle near her home in Machynlleth in Wales. Five days later, local man Mark Bridger was charged with her abduction and murder. This documentary tells the full story of what became the largest missing persons search in UK police history
