programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Years of bad luck and illness have left the Wrights unable to finish converting their bungalow in Ottery St Mary, east Devon, into a four-bedroom family home. With this in mind, Nick Knowles enlists the aid of local tradespeople and guest designer Charlie Luxton to help them complete the project

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Chesterfield, Derbyshire, to help Lisa and Richard Watson. With an expanding family and a young daughter who requires 24-hour care, builder Richard has not been able to complete an extension he started, but along with support from the building trade and local community, the team hopes to lift the strain on the family

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to a village in Conwy, north Wales, to help a widower finish building his home. Since the death of his wife and being diagnosed with cancer, David Williams has been unable to work on the property and lives in a caravan with his young daughter Erica - so the crew helps to make the house habitable with the support of the local community and traders

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Expats Dave and Sue arrive at the surgery after a 500-mile trip from France to bring their border collie Kaspar, who has a leg pain that is proving hard to diagnose. An eight-year-old pug needs surgery on its elbow, and an Egyptian Mau cat is rushed into Fitzpatricks after fracturing its knee in a fall from a second-floor balcony. Noel goes ahead with surgery on the complex fracture, but if there are any complications the kitten may face amputation

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of pioneering veterinary practice Fitzpatrick Referrals in Surrey, where orthopaedic-neuro veterinary surgeon Noel Fitzpatrick and his team help some of the nations hardest-to-cure pets using cutting-edge procedures. A three-year-old Newfoundland dog needs surgery for its bent front legs, and a long-haired Chihuahua requires a total hip replacement. A woman brings in her German shepherd, which has developed a painful degenerative spinal disease requiring treatment

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Years of bad luck and illness have left the Wrights unable to finish converting their bungalow in Ottery St Mary, east Devon, into a four-bedroom family home. With this in mind, Nick Knowles enlists the aid of local tradespeople and guest designer Charlie Luxton to help them complete the project

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Chesterfield, Derbyshire, to help Lisa and Richard Watson. With an expanding family and a young daughter who requires 24-hour care, builder Richard has not been able to complete an extension he started, but along with support from the building trade and local community, the team hopes to lift the strain on the family

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The response teams rush to save the life of a 21-year-old man who has been shot twice in the chest at a pub in Doncaster. Another man has a seizure high up in the cab of his tipper truck

programme.title: The Idaho Murders: Trial by TikTok
programme.desc: Zara McDermott visits Moscow, Idaho to uncover the story of the social media frenzy that followed the murders of four Idaho University students. She meets with online true crime sleuths building their own theories about the murders and those who were personally affected by the social media storm as she reflects on her own role in the tragedy and learns more about obsessive online consumption of true crime

programme.title: Psychopath with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: In 2007, when Paris Bennett was 13 years old, stabbed his four-year-old sister to death, a crime for which he has been behind bars ever since. Now 25 and in his first interview on British television, he tells Piers Morgan how this was no impulsive fit of rage but rather a cold, deliberate act planned as an act of revenge against his mother Charity - who is watching the interview unfold in a nearby room. She has stood by him even though she fears that when he gets out, he could still harbour violent intentions towards her - or even worse, towards his younger brother

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics in Oxford treat a woman suffering from a mystery illness, while in Portsmouth, the crews treat an elderly diabetic patient with dangerously low blood sugar

programme.title: Missing Persons Investigation
programme.desc: Two men go missing after a boys weekend goes terribly wrong, and detectives discover disturbing new details in a 20-year-old cold case of a missing teenager

programme.title: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
programme.desc: The rising trend of harmless images being turned into hardcore porn, footage that is often used against the people depicted with devastating consequences. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse, including a leading Florida State Senator, Lauren Book, the UK campaigner Kate Isaacs, and Dina, a woman who was deep-faked by a work colleague

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Years of bad luck and illness have left the Wrights unable to finish converting their bungalow in Ottery St Mary, east Devon, into a four-bedroom family home. With this in mind, Nick Knowles enlists the aid of local tradespeople and guest designer Charlie Luxton to help them complete the project

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Chesterfield, Derbyshire, to help Lisa and Richard Watson. With an expanding family and a young daughter who requires 24-hour care, builder Richard has not been able to complete an extension he started, but along with support from the building trade and local community, the team hopes to lift the strain on the family

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to a village in Conwy, north Wales, to help a widower finish building his home. Since the death of his wife and being diagnosed with cancer, David Williams has been unable to work on the property and lives in a caravan with his young daughter Erica - so the crew helps to make the house habitable with the support of the local community and traders

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Expats Dave and Sue arrive at the surgery after a 500-mile trip from France to bring their border collie Kaspar, who has a leg pain that is proving hard to diagnose. An eight-year-old pug needs surgery on its elbow, and an Egyptian Mau cat is rushed into Fitzpatricks after fracturing its knee in a fall from a second-floor balcony. Noel goes ahead with surgery on the complex fracture, but if there are any complications the kitten may face amputation

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of pioneering veterinary practice Fitzpatrick Referrals in Surrey, where orthopaedic-neuro veterinary surgeon Noel Fitzpatrick and his team help some of the nations hardest-to-cure pets using cutting-edge procedures. A three-year-old Newfoundland dog needs surgery for its bent front legs, and a long-haired Chihuahua requires a total hip replacement. A woman brings in her German shepherd, which has developed a painful degenerative spinal disease requiring treatment

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Years of bad luck and illness have left the Wrights unable to finish converting their bungalow in Ottery St Mary, east Devon, into a four-bedroom family home. With this in mind, Nick Knowles enlists the aid of local tradespeople and guest designer Charlie Luxton to help them complete the project

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Chesterfield, Derbyshire, to help Lisa and Richard Watson. With an expanding family and a young daughter who requires 24-hour care, builder Richard has not been able to complete an extension he started, but along with support from the building trade and local community, the team hopes to lift the strain on the family

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The response teams rush to save the life of a 21-year-old man who has been shot twice in the chest at a pub in Doncaster. Another man has a seizure high up in the cab of his tipper truck

programme.title: The Idaho Murders: Trial by TikTok
programme.desc: Zara McDermott visits Moscow, Idaho to uncover the story of the social media frenzy that followed the murders of four Idaho University students. She meets with online true crime sleuths building their own theories about the murders and those who were personally affected by the social media storm as she reflects on her own role in the tragedy and learns more about obsessive online consumption of true crime

programme.title: Psychopath with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: In 2007, when Paris Bennett was 13 years old, stabbed his four-year-old sister to death, a crime for which he has been behind bars ever since. Now 25 and in his first interview on British television, he tells Piers Morgan how this was no impulsive fit of rage but rather a cold, deliberate act planned as an act of revenge against his mother Charity - who is watching the interview unfold in a nearby room. She has stood by him even though she fears that when he gets out, he could still harbour violent intentions towards her - or even worse, towards his younger brother

programme.title: Missing Persons Investigation
programme.desc: A father disappears in crocodile-infested waters, while a young girl walks out of her family home and never returns

programme.title: Katie Piper: Locked Up in Louisiana
programme.desc: Katie meets Whitley, who was convicted of killing her brother after an argument. Whitley claims it was self-defence, but Katie learns she left the house and came back with a gun

programme.title: Deepfake Porn: Could You Be Next?
programme.desc: The rising trend of harmless images being turned into hardcore porn, footage that is often used against the people depicted with devastating consequences. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse, including a leading Florida State Senator, Lauren Book, the UK campaigner Kate Isaacs, and Dina, a woman who was deep-faked by a work colleague

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis goes off-grid as he spends time in the company of the adventurer and television presenter Bear Grylls, travelling with him to his private island off the coast of Wales where he is staying for the summer with his wife, Shara. Louis later accompanies Bear on trips to a scout troop in south London and a Marine reserve training camp, before the pair head to Devon where Bear is guest of honour at his own family adventure festival

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents a guide to the lives of plants in environments around the world, beginning with the plant battlegrounds of the tropical rainforests. New filming techniques allow viewers to enter the plants world and see it from their perspective, and on their timescale. Featuring fast-growing trees, flowers that mimic dead animals, and life and death battles to absorb much-needed sunlight

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: On the eve of his wedding, Bob turns down the chance of going on a drunken rampage in favour of a quiet night in. Classic comedy, starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A low-mileage Renault Dauphine proves irresistible to the team, but its 1959 mechanics ensure some hefty bills

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team, including Andy Joynson, buy the right kind of Land Rover, but its got the wrong engine. Can they clean up its emissions with a modern motor?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures once more on to the European rail network to retrace journeys featured in George Bradshaws 1913 publication Continental Guide, beginning by travelling through Russia. He starts in the industrial city of Tula, before visiting Leo Tolstoys former home of Yasnaya Polyana and learning how the authors life and works were intertwined with the railways. Michael then boards the train that runs from the Caspian Sea to Moscow, where he performs an important role in a dramatic opera at the Bolshoi Theatre, before exploring the beauty and history of St Petersburg and riding on the first railway ever built in Russia

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Steered by his 1913 Bradshaw railway guide, Michael Portillo takes the train down the spine of Italy as he travels from Rome to Sicily. He begins by weaving among the capital citys landmarks on the back of a 1950s Vespa, before boarding the train south to Naples, where he finds out about the first railway to be built in the country, and ventures into the crater of Mount Vesuvius. He takes a detour to the island of Capri, before finishing his journey in the ancient hilltop town of Taormina

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team purchase a 1950s Austin Westminster rally car in the hope that seasoned competitor Dave Mathewson can win a race

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A rare Reliant Scimitar GTC undergoes an expert recommission that leaves no bolt unturned, and a Honda CBR superbike from the 1980s needs a repair

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates an account of the liberation of Paris and Brussels in 1944, events which to many signified the end of the war. However, as the German forces retreated, Poland fell victim to one of the most tragic events of the war, Documentary chronicling the Second World War

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The search for Josef Mengele, one of the most wanted Nazi war criminals, which was led by Holocaust survivor Simon Wiesenthal

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Ebrington Square in Londonderry, where highlights include a Celtic stone head that could be thousands of years old, rare Belleek vases bought for just a few pounds from a charity shop, and a drawing by Muhammad Ali. Plus, Mark Hill gets the chance to see a childs model Austin Pathfinder pedal car and Fiona gets a history lesson about Northern Irish motor racing when she finds out about the Crossle car company

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Crystal Palace Park in London. Highlights include rare car club badges, including one from the earliest days of motoring in Iraq, My Little Pony toys, and a work by LS Lowry that was owned by a woman who befriended the artist at the end of his life. Serhat Ahmet challenges Fiona to identify the most valuable porcelain cup in a trial dubbed High Tea or Low Tea

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson collects a Jaguar Mark 2 and explains why they made such poor getaway cars for 1960s bank robbers. A customer considers a Morris Marina as a Christmas present

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team is under pressure with a record number of cars to auction, but Derek finds room for one more - a replica AC Cobra. A couple are reunited with their Volvo breakdown truck

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The search for Josef Mengele, one of the most wanted Nazi war criminals, which was led by Holocaust survivor Simon Wiesenthal

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates an account of the liberation of Paris and Brussels in 1944, events which to many signified the end of the war. However, as the German forces retreated, Poland fell victim to one of the most tragic events of the war, Documentary chronicling the Second World War

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: A polar bear hunts with her cubs, while the warmer weather of spring accelerates a process that will eventually see an area of ice the size of Australia disappear into the Arctic Ocean. A woolly bear caterpillar emerges from the snow after spending the winter frozen, and in Antarctica male Adelie penguins arrive to begin constructing nests in fierce storms as they try to attract mates

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: How animals in the polar regions adapt to the summer weather. In a colony of 400,000 king penguins the adults go surfing and the chicks take mud-baths to cool off, while polar bear cubs take their first swimming lessons as the ice they inhabit melts away. A minke whale is hunted by a family of killer whales, succumbing to their battering after two hours, and Adelie penguins try to prevent their chicks being snatched by skuas

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Adelaide to Perth: The Indian Pacific
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on an adventure aboard the transcontinental Indian Pacific Railway, from Adelaide to Perth. Along the way he hears the story of an enterprising 19th-century Briton with a murky past who made his money growing vines, strikes it lucky in one of the most lucrative goldfields in the world and pays a flying visit to the doctor to hear how patients in remote Western Australia are cared for. He also hears how Edward VIII was caught up in a train accident near Bridgetown and learns about a child migration programme that cast a dark shadow on the history of both the UK and Australia

programme.title: Locomotion: Dan Snows History of Railways
programme.desc: The historian and broadcaster examines the impact the railway had on London in the late 1830s, linking it to Birmingham, Liverpool and Manchester. This was the start of a truly national network and one of the greatest civil engineering projects in history, while tycoons including Samuel Morton Peto and George Hudson made fortunes as the stock markets boomed. However, the bubble burst in 1847 and shares plummeted, leaving thousands of people facing bankruptcy courts

programme.title: Planet Earth III
programme.desc: David Attenborough showcases wildlife in coastal regions, from Cape fur seals on n South Africas Robberg Peninsula to hungry lions on Namibias infamous Skeleton Coast. Plus, a look at how the Arctic coast is the scene of the biggest seasonal transformation on Earth, as the melting of billions of tonnes of ice brings short-lived opportunities to coastal waters

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents a guide to the world of plants that grow in water, including a bubbling river in Brazil, where the plants have created their own atmosphere and a lake smothered in brightly-coloured flowers. Plus, plants that fight each other for growing space or use natural adhesives to prevent themselves being swept downstream, and a look at the methods used to evade or even attack animals

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: British car enthusiasts Will and Gus buy a Porsche 923 with a cracked interior, bad paintwork and ripped seats. This driving legend is well past its best, but they still hope to turn a profit

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien assess sewing machines in Kent as they continue their journey across the UK to find unique and valuable items lost in peoples homes

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: Prawn boat Amitys engines are on the brink of failing, and skipper Jimmy Buchan has to decide whether to cut loose the £30,000 nets to ensure a safe return to port. Meanwhile, newcomer Ryan Morrison tries to impress his boss on a trawler with a reputation for success

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: Prawn boat Amity gets caught in a winter storm 100 miles from shore, and is in danger of sinking while its nets are stuck on the seabed. Elsewhere, a rival trawler with a young skipper and crew is having great success. Series following the lives of Peterhead fishermen working in the North Sea

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaws Continental Railway Guide to explore Poland. He begins in the capital Warsaw, where he takes to the floor to dance to one of Chopins polonaises with high-school students rehearsing for their leavers ball, before discovering how the former industrial city of Lodz supplied the vast Russian empire of the early 20th century. He then heads to Poznan and rides one of the few remaining steam-powered commuter trains, visits a factory in Wroclaw that manufactures car bodies for locomotives, and ends his journey in Krakow, where he takes a tour in an iconic vehicle of the communist era

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: The historian examines four dramatic chapters in British history, exploring how changing attitudes to gender politics, class inequality, mental health and children can challenge perceptions of the past and provide new answers to each mystery. She begins with the 16th-century witch hunts, when thousands of ordinary people, mostly women, were hunted down, tortured and killed across Scotland and England

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team are on the hunt to restore one of the greatest names in motoring history - the Bugatti. But with a strict budget they settle for a replica

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team takes on a modern day fast Ford to see if a quick flip could make a few quid and revisit a 3 year MG restoration project thats still not finished

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The story of the Nazi quest to establish an Aryan race, which set out to subjugate the Jewish population with decrees and laws about citizenship and escalated to the use of execution camps and the horrors of the Holocaust. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The Jewish group the Avengers who, as the Second World War was ending, hunted down persecutors known to have contributed to deaths during the Holocaust, including Wilhelm Stuckart

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team are in Roundhay Park, Leeds. Elaine Binning is thrilled to find a psychedelic plastic chair from the 1960s while Susan Rumfitt admires a diamond ring that could be from the 17th century. Paul Atterbury hears the tale of the London bus that jumped over Tower Bridge and Clive Farahar sees a collection of family photos that documents a visit to Leeds by Arnold Schwarzenegger during his early bodybuilding days and an original script for Dads Army

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Pollok Park in Glasgow. Treasures include an early painting by Jack Vettriano, a Ming dynasty Buddha, a harmonica that once belonged to Johnny Cash, and pages from the very first printing presses. Jewellery specialist Ishy Khan shows Fiona two stunning rings, one sapphire and one tourmaline, and challenges her to work out which one is most valuable

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.sub-title: Red E Type and Fast Fords
programme.desc: A classic car fan with a love of the Jaguar E-Type reluctantly sends his dream car to auction, hoping to buy a motorhome with the money. Two Ford Escorts get Daves pulse racing

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A widow reluctantly parts with her husbands treasured collection of Rolls-Royces and Bentleys, and an impulse buyer finds one of the cars a new home

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The story of the Nazi quest to establish an Aryan race, which set out to subjugate the Jewish population with decrees and laws about citizenship and escalated to the use of execution camps and the horrors of the Holocaust. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The Jewish group the Avengers who, as the Second World War was ending, hunted down persecutors known to have contributed to deaths during the Holocaust, including Wilhelm Stuckart

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: With the arrival of autumn the Arctic Ocean begins freezing over, and polar bears gather in large numbers on the coast as they await the return of the ice. Two thousand beluga whales head for a particular estuary to thrash against the gravel and exfoliate, while in Antarctica, Adelie penguins take to the sea for the first time and face the danger of leopard seal attacks

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines how the arrival of winter sparks a ruthless battle for survival in the polar regions, with temperatures plummeting to -70C, winds reaching 120mph and day-long darkness. As a blizzard rages, a polar bear treks into the Arctic mountains to give birth, while the entire world population of spectacled eider braves the weather in a giant ice hole. Wolves and bison engage in battle, but alliances are also made, and beneath the snow, voles try to dodge the attacks of great grey owls and weasels

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Sunderland to pick up a real Italian beauty - a 1972 Lancia Fulvia Zagato. Red and racy, the car has seen better days, but Dereks still impressed

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek acquires a collection of pre and post war classics, including a Vincent Rapide, a 1928 Alvis, a Ford Pilot and an Indian Scout with sidecar

programme.title: Planet Earth III
programme.desc: The wildlife inhabiting the worlds oceans, from the shallow seas of the tropics, where predators like the lionfish can become the prey to one of the worlds oddest hunters, to the greatest depths, where a massive siphonophore, longer than a blue whale, and a gulper eel with huge jaws are captured on film using specialised vessels designed to withstand the pressure. The episode also features the mating dances of mobula rays and the symbiotic relationship between Columbus crabs and turtles

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: The strategy, deception and feats of engineering plants use to thrive in the changing weather of different seasons. In the face of conditions ranging from ice and snow to raging fires, survival is often a question of perfect timing - particularly when contending with intense competition and surprising predators

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: British car enthusiasts Will and Gus lay their hands on a potentially very valuable Volvo. Its great to look at, but doesnt start easily and cannot stop, requiring an overhaul of the ignition and breaks

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole tries to restore a monkey bike, and Simon OBrien makes use of all his skills as he tries to give new life to an old racing bicycle

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: A vital chain gives way on the Ocean Ventures nets and Barry Lauder has to think quickly to save the day. Meanwhile on the Amity, skipper Jimmy Buchan ignores the concerns of his first mate and enters the jagged contours of the Ooze Hole to fish for prawns

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: The skipper of one of the smallest boats in the Peterhead fleet must decide whether to head back to harbour or brave the treacherous sea following a severe weather warning. Two trawlers who fish as a pair risk disaster as they cast their shared nets on stormy waters, and James Thores desperation to find haddock sees him act on a potentially costly hunch

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaws Continental Railway Guide to explore Poland. He then heads to Poznan and rides one of the few remaining steam-powered commuter trains, visits a factory in Wroclaw that manufactures car bodies for locomotives, and ends his journey in Krakow, where he takes a tour in an iconic vehicle of the communist era

programme.title: Lucy Worsley Investigates
programme.desc: The historian examines four dramatic chapters in British history, exploring how changing attitudes to gender politics, class inequality, mental health and children can challenge perceptions of the past and provide new answers to each mystery. She begins with the 16th-century witch hunts, when thousands of ordinary people, mostly women, were hunted down, tortured and killed across Scotland and England

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team are on the hunt to restore one of the greatest names in motoring history - the Bugatti. But with a strict budget they settle for a replica

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team takes on a modern day fast Ford to see if a quick flip could make a few quid and revisit a 3 year MG restoration project thats still not finished

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The story of the Nazi quest to establish an Aryan race, which set out to subjugate the Jewish population with decrees and laws about citizenship and escalated to the use of execution camps and the horrors of the Holocaust. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The Jewish group the Avengers who, as the Second World War was ending, hunted down persecutors known to have contributed to deaths during the Holocaust, including Wilhelm Stuckart

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team are in Roundhay Park, Leeds. Elaine Binning is thrilled to find a psychedelic plastic chair from the 1960s while Susan Rumfitt admires a diamond ring that could be from the 17th century. Paul Atterbury hears the tale of the London bus that jumped over Tower Bridge and Clive Farahar sees a collection of family photos that documents a visit to Leeds by Arnold Schwarzenegger during his early bodybuilding days and an original script for Dads Army

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Pollok Park in Glasgow. Treasures include an early painting by Jack Vettriano, a Ming dynasty Buddha, a harmonica that once belonged to Johnny Cash, and pages from the very first printing presses. Jewellery specialist Ishy Khan shows Fiona two stunning rings, one sapphire and one tourmaline, and challenges her to work out which one is most valuable

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.sub-title: Red E Type and Fast Fords
programme.desc: A classic car fan with a love of the Jaguar E-Type reluctantly sends his dream car to auction, hoping to buy a motorhome with the money. Two Ford Escorts get Daves pulse racing

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A widow reluctantly parts with her husbands treasured collection of Rolls-Royces and Bentleys, and an impulse buyer finds one of the cars a new home

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The story of the Nazi quest to establish an Aryan race, which set out to subjugate the Jewish population with decrees and laws about citizenship and escalated to the use of execution camps and the horrors of the Holocaust. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The Jewish group the Avengers who, as the Second World War was ending, hunted down persecutors known to have contributed to deaths during the Holocaust, including Wilhelm Stuckart

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: With the arrival of autumn the Arctic Ocean begins freezing over, and polar bears gather in large numbers on the coast as they await the return of the ice. Two thousand beluga whales head for a particular estuary to thrash against the gravel and exfoliate, while in Antarctica, Adelie penguins take to the sea for the first time and face the danger of leopard seal attacks

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines how the arrival of winter sparks a ruthless battle for survival in the polar regions, with temperatures plummeting to -70C, winds reaching 120mph and day-long darkness. As a blizzard rages, a polar bear treks into the Arctic mountains to give birth, while the entire world population of spectacled eider braves the weather in a giant ice hole. Wolves and bison engage in battle, but alliances are also made, and beneath the snow, voles try to dodge the attacks of great grey owls and weasels

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Sunderland to pick up a real Italian beauty - a 1972 Lancia Fulvia Zagato. Red and racy, the car has seen better days, but Dereks still impressed

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek acquires a collection of pre and post war classics, including a Vincent Rapide, a 1928 Alvis, a Ford Pilot and an Indian Scout with sidecar

programme.title: Planet Earth III
programme.desc: The wildlife inhabiting the worlds oceans, from the shallow seas of the tropics, where predators like the lionfish can become the prey to one of the worlds oddest hunters, to the greatest depths, where a massive siphonophore, longer than a blue whale, and a gulper eel with huge jaws are captured on film using specialised vessels designed to withstand the pressure. The episode also features the mating dances of mobula rays and the symbiotic relationship between Columbus crabs and turtles

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents a guide to the world of plants that grow in water, including a bubbling river in Brazil, where the plants have created their own atmosphere and a lake smothered in brightly-coloured flowers. Plus, plants that fight each other for growing space or use natural adhesives to prevent themselves being swept downstream, and a look at the methods used to evade or even attack animals

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: British car enthusiasts Will and Gus splash out £550 on a garage owners Mercedes 190. Its previous owner has failed to fix it, but the duo are more confident

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole tries to restore a monkey bike, and Simon OBrien makes use of all his skills as he tries to give new life to an old racing bicycle

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: A vital chain gives way on the Ocean Ventures nets and Barry Lauder has to think quickly to save the day. Meanwhile on the Amity, skipper Jimmy Buchan ignores the concerns of his first mate and enters the jagged contours of the Ooze Hole to fish for prawns

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: The skipper of one of the smallest boats in the Peterhead fleet must decide whether to head back to harbour or brave the treacherous sea following a severe weather warning. Two trawlers who fish as a pair risk disaster as they cast their shared nets on stormy waters, and James Thores desperation to find haddock sees him act on a potentially costly hunch

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Vanderpump Rules
programme.desc: Raquels birthday dreams come true when she embarks on a glamping trip with some of her closest friends.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade ventures into Brazils cattle country as he searches for a deadly electric eel that has killed three cowboys. To catch his elusive prey he has to combat all his fears, and after a long chase he finally corners it in an unexpected place

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Sister Brigid helps secure a future for Moira and her baby, but when events take a shocking turn Weatherill and Ormerod are left to make a heartbreaking decision. Susie tries her best to be a model nurse and breaks off her relationship with Carnegie - who then proposes to her - while Bobby takes matters into his own hands with the violent stepfather of a local thug. Linda Armstrong, Amy Robbins, Robert Daws and Sarah Beck Mather star

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs - A Royal Special
programme.desc: Battersea Dogs and Cats Home is increasingly taking in dogs that are hard to rehome because of their age, their behaviour or their medical issues. In this special programme, Paul is joined by Camilla, Queen Consort, then the Duchess of Cornwall, to help these dogs in a variety of ways, from teaching a blind one how to find its way in the world, to bathing and comforting dogs that have been found abandoned on the streets. Paul also takes dogs to a Garden Party hosted by Her Majesty at Clarence House in the hope of finding them their homes in time for Christmas

programme.desc: After a mystery sea monster washes up on a UK beach, extreme angler Jeremy Wade sets off on a journey to unmask this beast and find out whether it poses any threat to humans. programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell report on two more cases. More than 40 years ago, Caroline Kinsey was pregnant, alone and unable to provide the kind of family and upbringing she believed all children deserved. Burdened by her heart-breaking decision to give her son up for adoption, Caroline longs to find him, to hear that he did have a happy childhood, and to know whether he can forgive her after all these years. Raised as an only child, Paul McFarlanes beloved mother died when he was nine, but years later, a single piece of paper threw all his assumptions about his small family into question

Fact-based drama, starring Stephen Graham

programme.desc: The plight of two young girls is brought to the attention of Carol, who discovers them home alone. Joe confronts their parents who are out drinking, but finds stepfather Brian unremorseful and reluctant to leave the pub. Gina is distracted from her worries about her unborn baby by preparations for the imminent arrival of Phils niece Dawn, but when David goes to meet the girl from the station she is nowhere to be seen. Nikki Sanderson joins the cast

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detectives curiosity is aroused by the passengers on board a luxury cruise ship bound for Egypt - particularly a woman who appears inordinately offensive to her fellow travellers. programme.title: My Unique BandB
programme.desc: Simon Parfett and his team help create a unique agricultural-themed BandB for Will and his family, who have been farming their land in Lincolnshire for generations

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The work of Liverpools Walton Centre, the countrys only specialist neurology hospital trust, as staff deal with patients suffering from the complex brain condition FND (Functional Neurological Disorder). Sixty-six-year-old Pauline has been left severely disabled by FND but there is nothing physically wrong with her body. David, a 38-year-old window cleaner, has been unable to walk more than a few steps a year. However, medical professionals are still learning about the condition - so the Walton Centre started an FND service in 2012

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: This Time Next Year

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi pays a visit to Devons historic Churston Court. Chris Hodson and Jonathan Smith fell in love with the old building with their hearts rather their heads, and five months in the pair now realise the amount of work needed to bring the hotel into the 21st century. Alex arrives a night early to find a cluttered, dark decor with no one on reception and quickly realises she needs to help motivate Chris to change the way he manages his team. Can she reverse decades of ugly decor, teach her hosts to clear the clutter, erase the oppressive red and herald a more glamorous, curated look?

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The irascible copper finds his methods are called into question when a colleague is seriously injured during an attempt to thwart a suicide. With David Jason, Philip Jackson, Jeff Rawle and Danny Webb

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The documentary looks at Alder Hey Childrens Hospitals craniofacial department - one of only four in the UK that treats life-threatening congenital skull and facial abnormalities. With a rise in referrals, the department is over-subscribed and neurosurgeon Chris Parks, who leads the craniofacial surgical team, faces a non-stop workload. Chris always works hand in glove with Ben Robertson, one of three paediatric maxillofacial surgeons in the UK. But this partnership is about to be broken up, as Ben is leaving to return to his native Australia. It takes 23 years training to become a maxillofacial Consultant specialising in cranio-surgery, and there is currently nobody experienced enough to replace Ben, putting extra pressure on the waiting lists

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi sets out to reverse the fortunes of the Grove Arms in Wiltshire. Three years ago, Nina Bartlett gave up a successful legal career in the City to fulfil her dream of running a dog-friendly village pub. However, despite her best efforts and last years good turnover, the Grove in Ludwell is currently making a loss, leaving her unsure which way to turn. Nina admits she is afraid to rely on her own staff, which leaves her doing the bulk of the work herself and on the brink of burnout. Alex takes Nina a lesson in getting the basics right, but does the former law specialist have the energy to see this through?

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as Charles from Basildon, Ruth from Leeds, Pam from Nottingham and Aidan from Coventry work as a team to take on one of the experts in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host as four new stars - reality TV star Ekin-Su Culculoglu, actor and comedian Hugh Dennis, author and interiors expert Kelly Hoppen, and former footballer Shaun Wright-Phillips - and their mentors, cook for their loved ones. Theyll have to produce delicious dishes with the added pressure of not only impressing the chefs, but their friends and family who will also be in the studio including Hughs friend David Baddiel

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.sub-title: Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, Shobna Gulati, David James and Will Bayley
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, Shobna Gulati, David James and Will Bayley take on quiz experts Mark Labbett, Anne Hegerty, Shaun Wallace, Paul Sinha, Jenny Ryan and Darragh Ennis, hoping to win big money for their chosen charities

programme.title: Olivia Attwood: The Price of Perfection
programme.desc: Self-confessed surgery addict Olivia explores Generation Zs obsession with cosmetic surgery in an eye-opening and immersive series where she meet experts and beauty-obsessed contributors before trying out the latest fads - from vampire breast lifts to laser vaginal tightening - herself

programme.title: Monday with Gavan Reilly
programme.desc: Gavan Reilly and guests break down the week?s political diary, key Cabinet issues, major stories shaping the national agenda, and the impact of upcoming decisions.

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines more real-life cases, beginning with the 2001 murder of 15-year-old Danielle Jones, who went missing on her way to school

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder tells the story of Frances Molloy, whose 18-year-old son was killed in a coach crash in 2012, leading her to fight for changes to the law, making it illegal for buses and coaches to use tyres that are more than 10 years old. He also examines the 1973 case of David McGreavy, who murdered three children he was supposed to be looking after
